# Warden

> Warden is a local code review CLI that runs deterministic tools (tsc, eslint, npm audit), verifies every external claim through citable sources (OSV, .d.ts), and uses an LLM only as a triage and formatting layer. The CLI returns a stable comment contract; the model never invents assertions.

## Pages

- [Home](https://wrdn.beauty/): Hero, thesis, pipeline, comment anatomy.
- [Docs · Overview](https://wrdn.beauty/docs/): What Warden checks and what it explicitly does not.
- [Docs · Install](https://wrdn.beauty/docs/install/): How to install and run the CLI locally.
- [Docs · Configuration](https://wrdn.beauty/docs/configuration/): Environment variables and review modes.
- [Docs · Reading comments](https://wrdn.beauty/docs/reading-comments/): How to read the tier/category/source shape of a comment.
- [Docs · CI usage](https://wrdn.beauty/docs/ci/): Wiring Warden into a CI step.
- [Examples](https://wrdn.beauty/examples/): A rendered fixture from Warden reviewing its own codebase.
- [Design](https://wrdn.beauty/design/): Links into decisions.md, vision.md, and CONTEXT.md.
- [Notes](https://wrdn.beauty/notes/): Longer-form product and architecture writing.
- [Notes · The reviewer should bring receipts](https://wrdn.beauty/notes/reviewer-with-receipts/): The product thesis.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/99Yash/warden)

- [CONTEXT.md (noun glossary)](https://github.com/99Yash/warden/blob/main/CONTEXT.md)
